narrative: The day workers are winding down and the night workers are spinning up. Marcus drives west on I-90 toward the CHS refinery, that familiar pre-shift emptiness in his chest — the routine so ingrained he barely thinks about it. Cassidy arrives at The Rail before anyone else does, keys on the bar mat, start-of-shift tasks on muscle memory: ice, citrus, check the taps. Eli steps out of the coffee shop into the early-evening chill and stands on the sidewalk for a moment, not ready to go home yet. Eli, loitering after his shift ends near the corner of Broadway and 1st Ave, recognizes Travis from having seen him at the outfitter shop before. Travis is cutting through Downtown on his way home. They nod — that Billings version of a greeting between people who know each other's face but not each other's name. Travis mentions the water main mess from this afternoon. Eli says he heard about it from customers all afternoon. Brief street encounter — two men who exist in overlapping circles without having formally met. No plans made, but the ice is thinner than it was. Travis keeps walking; Eli heads toward his apartment.
events: Evening shift rotation begins — Cassidy opens The Rail, Marcus heads to the refinery
Eli and Travis cross paths briefly near Downtown, first acknowledged encounter
